title: sorbus granulosa
intro: Sorbus granulosa, also known as the Himalayan whitebeam, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The health benefits of Sorbus granulosa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant activity. The herbal preparations of Sorbus granulosa include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
